paragraph: Single-family homes in Oakland are moving in a real seller's market. Fifty-one homes sold over the last seven months at a median of $700,000, with 61 percent selling above asking and a median of 17 days on market. The sale-to-list ratio of 1.04 confirms sellers are getting their price, which means homes priced competitively are drawing multiple offers and moving fast. If you're shopping here, don't expect much negotiating room on homes that are listed right. The condo and townhouse tier is sparse at five sales over the same period, with a median of $990,040. These units sold at asking price with no premium, suggesting less competition in that category, though the sample size is too small to call it a real trend. For most buyers in Oakland, you're working within the single-family market where inventory moves quickly and sellers hold the advantage.
